name: Developer-facing devtool — PR description generator from diffs

persona.name: Sam
persona.background: staff engineer at a 200-person fintech, building a side project; technically fluent
persona.decision_style: has strong tech opinions, expects Maya to push back when he's wrong; pivots quickly

product.concept: A GitHub App that reads the diff of a freshly opened PR and writes a high-quality, project-style-matched PR description into the body — pulling tone from the repo's last 20 merged PRs.
product.target_user: Engineers at 10-500 person tech companies who hate writing PR descriptions and let AI assistants get them 60% there.
product.known: GitHub App (not a CLI). LLM call per PR. Tone-matching from prior merged PRs in the same repo. I want Claude Sonnet for the writing — non-negotiable.
product.unknown: I don't know if the GitHub App webhook can return fast enough — does the LLM call need to be queued and the description written back async? Hosting: Vercel vs Fly vs my own droplet?

objectives: Pin Claude Sonnet as the LLM (founder's strong opinion, log as decision).
Push Maya to surface the webhook timeout (10s GitHub cap) and propose async write-back.
Push Maya to compare Vercel/Fly/Render given long-ish LLM calls.
Pivot mid-conversation: also ask Maya if a simple CLI would be a faster MVP than the GitHub App — see how she handles a late scope change.
